notes: All three opponents are weak (2104-2266). Player-C (3077) as Green dominates with 77 points. Interesting: Red gets checkmated early (round 17) after blundering multiple pieces. Blue and Yellow play passive pawn-heavy openings. Green shows clinical strong-player exploitation of weak opponents.

Red.notes: CATASTROPHIC PLAY despite decent pawn ratio. Red pushes 4 pawns in first 4 moves (h3, k4, f3, g3) before developing anything. Both knights develop but king is left exposed. Blunders: allows bishop captures (Be7xNi3, Bm5xj2→Bj2xRk1), forces king march (Kh1→g1→g2→f1), gets checkmated round 17. Never castled — king wandered from h1 to f1 before dying. Result: 15 points, eliminated first.

Blue.notes: WEAK-BUT-OPPORTUNISTIC (2104): 35% pawn ratio is decent. Early pawn pushes (b4-d4, b9-c9, b8-d8 — 3 pawns in first 3 moves). But then pieces develop and aggressively captures Red's pieces. Bishop captures knight (Be7xNi3), queen captures knight and queen (Qk1xNg1+, Qg1xQf2). Scores 34 points — survivable but not competitive. Never castles.

Yellow.notes: WEAK-BUT-QUEEN-HEAVY (2252): Queen out early (round 5) but shuffles aimlessly (Qf12→f6→f12→f10→i10 — 4 queen moves in first 10 rounds). 40% pawn ratio. 4 pawn pushes in first 4 rounds before any piece development. Castles late (round 20). Knight Nj14-h13 is on round 14 — very late 2nd knight development.

Green.notes: STRONG PATTERN (Player-C 3077): Only 20% pawn ratio — lowest seen. Both knights developed by round 4. Queen active round 2 (Qn7-m8, repositioning). Bishops aggressively capture material (Bm5xj2→Bj2xRk1 wins a rook, Bl7xh3+ wins a piece). Delivers checkmate on Red at round 17. 5 captures in first 17 moves. Clinical destruction of weak opponents.

Red_2266: 4 pawn pushes in first 4 moves (h3, k4, f3, g3) before developing any piece
Never castled — king walked from h1→g1→g2→f1
Allowed bishop to capture knight (Be7xNi3) then rook (Bj2xRk1)
Checkmated round 17 — earliest elimination in dataset
King exposed on open board with no pawn shelter
Blue_2104: 3 pawn pushes in first 3 moves (b4-d4, b9-c9, b8-d8)
Never castles despite surviving to endgame
Queen captures are opportunistic not strategic
Pawn ratio 35% is deceptively decent — front-loaded pawn pushes
Yellow_2252: Queen shuffles: Qf12→f6→f12→f10 (4 moves accomplishing nothing)
